title: Budget & Performance
description: Documentation of how DHS performs its mission and provides stewardship of resources: Evaluation & Evidence Plans, DHS Budget, Hiring Excellence.
text:
Reports required by the Committees on Appropriations of the Senate and the House of Representatives, from 2015 to present.
A listing of budget documents from FY 2003 to present, including budget in briefs, fact sheets, overviews, and links to OMB overviews.
Evaluation and evidence-building plans outline areas for evidence to improve program and policy effectiveness, assess progress toward outcomes, and inform resource management.
The U.S. Department of Homeland Security is establishing hiring excellence in attracting, recruiting and retaining the best people.
Department of Homeland Security is committed to Open Government. We are working to create a culture of transparency, participation, and collaboration in government operations.
Performance and financial reports provide information that enables the President, Congress and the public to assess mission performance and management of resources.
The Department of Homeland Security is dedicated to planning and executing DHS conferences as cost-effectively and efficiently as possible. This page provides the Department’s annual report of all agency-sponsored conferences where the net expenses exceed $100,000.
In an effort to standardize IT resources, reduce costs, and improve efficiency, DHS created a program to consolidate the numerous computing facilities currently supporting each of the DHS Components.
